structural_claim: Symmetry principles dictate the structure of interactions and constrain the possible dynamics, with the symmetry group determining both conserved quantities and the form of the governing equations

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Local gauge symmetry uniquely determines the form of fundamental interactions through the requirement of local symmetry invariance
All fundamental forces arise from a single gauge principle based on symmetry groups
Axisymmetry reduces the three-dimensional equilibrium problem to a single two-dimensional equation through symmetry constraints
Gyro-symmetry allows averaging over fast timescales while preserving essential physics, with the symmetry determining the reduced description
